title: From attosecond FEL simulation to high-frequency market microstructure
research_question: Can the numerical methods used to study attosecond free-electron-laser pulse generation at SwissFEL be transferred to a reproducible limit-order-book laboratory for studying liquidity, delay, queue dynamics, and shock recovery?
hypothesis: A thesis-style workflow based on noise-driven dynamics, delay control, taper-like parameter optimisation, and seed-robust validation can expose how latency-sensitive liquidity provision changes spread, imbalance, volatility, and post-shock resilience.

research_features.thesis_title: Numerical Study of the Attosecond Free-Electron Laser Pulse Generation in the Soft X-ray Regime at the SwissFEL
research_features.doctoral_angle: EPFL PhD thesis: numerical study of attosecond FEL pulse trains at SwissFEL Athos using SASE/ESASE, slicing, mode-locking, transverse gradient undulator configuration, slippage analysis, superradiance interpretation, and undulator taper optimisation.
research_features.career_angle: High-frequency quant transition project: translate the thesis strengths into exchange simulation, noise-started order-flow dynamics, queue-aware microstructure features, delay/latency stress tests, and reproducible parameter sweeps.
research_features.mechanism: Transient information shock plus adaptive liquidity replenishment, interpreted through FEL-inspired concepts: SASE-like noise start, ESASE-like current spikes, slicing-like selective resonance, mode-locking-like delay control, and TGU-like strong taper optimisation.
